rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not invent new declarations.
Do not generate AGREE expressions that reference AADL properties like Stack_Size unless they are explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions: numeric variables must have numeric expressions, and Boolean variables must have Boolean expressions.
Move implementation-level bindings to the correct implementation annex and avoid placing them in component type contracts.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, always validate that referenced identifiers exist in the current scope.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Ensure all AGREE expressions conform to the expected scalar types (int, real, bool) as defined by the AADL model.
Prefer minimal repairs that preserve existing architecture and avoid adding new components or ports.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions/guarantees involving ports; implementation annexes are for bindings.
Helper functions and constants must be declared before use and match the types of their expressions.
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex to avoid resolution errors.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by adding new architecture elements or property associations.
Avoid generating AGREE expressions that reference AADL property names like Stack_Size directly as variables.
Do not assume that all AADL identifiers are automatically available in AGREE scope without explicit declaration or visibility.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about missing package references
Requirements related to signal routing in implementation layer
